lessons.title: Testamentary Capacity
lessons.description: The governing statute is the Succession Act 1965 — one of the most important pieces of legislation in Irish property law.

lessons.title: Revocation Codicils and Alteration
lessons.description: A will is by nature ambulatory — it speaks from death, not from the date it is made.

lessons.title: Construction of Wills
lessons.description: The governing principle in construction of wills is the testator's intention — what did the testator mean?

lessons.title: Legal Right Share Section 111
lessons.description: Under section 111 of the Succession Act 1965, where a testator leaves a spouse and children, the spouse's legal right share is one third of the estate.

lessons.title: Provision for Children Section 117
lessons.description: Section 117(1) of the Succession Act 1965 provides that where a testator fails in their moral duty to make proper provision for a child in accordance with their means, the court may order such provision to be made from the estate as the court thinks just.

lessons.title: Intestacy
lessons.description: There are two types of intestacy. Total intestacy arises where the deceased has left no valid will at all.
